{{神社| 名称=琴崎八幡宮| 所在地=山口県宇部市上宇部大小路| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=品陀和気命
気長足比女命
足仲津比古命| 社格=県社・別表神社| 創建=[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）| 例祭=[[10月15日]]}} '''琴崎八幡宮'''（ことざきはちまんぐう）は、[[山口県]][[宇部市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。宇部市の総鎮守として崇敬を受ける。 [[八幡神]]（[[応神天皇|品陀和気命]]・[[神功皇后|気長足比女命]]・[[仲哀天皇|足仲津比古命]]）を主[[祭神]]とし、[[宗像三女神]]（多紀理比女命・多紀津比女命・[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島比女命]]）を配祀する。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）、行教が[[宇佐神宮|宇佐]]から[[石清水八幡宮|石清水]]へ八幡神を勧請する途中で宇部郷琴芝の浦に行宮が設けられ、その跡地に村人が八幡神の分霊を祀ったのが当社の起源であるという。[[天授]]3年（[[1377年]]）、[[神託]]により現在地の琴崎に遷座した。歴代領主の[[厚東氏]]・[[大内氏]]・[[毛利氏]]らの崇敬を受けた。 明治6年に郷社に列し、昭和13年に県社に昇格した。昭和41年7月、神社本庁の別表神社に加列された。 ==外部リンク== *[http://kotozaki.com 琴崎八幡宮]（公式サイト） [[Category:山口県の神社|ことさきはちまんくう]] [[Category:府県社|ことさきはちまんくう]] [[Category:別表神社|ことさきはちまんくう]] [[Category:八幡宮|ことさきはちまんくう]] {{shinto-stub}}

足仲津比古命 |社格=県社・別表神社 |創建=[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]） |例祭=[[10月15日]] }} '''琴崎八幡宮'''（ことざきはちまんぐう）は、[[山口県]][[宇部市]]にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。宇部市の総鎮守として崇敬を受ける。 [[八幡神]]（[[応神天皇|品陀和気命]]・[[神功皇后|気長足比女命]]・[[仲哀天皇|足仲津比古命]]）を主[[祭神]]とし、[[宗像三女神]]（多紀理比女命・多紀津比女命・[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島比女命]]）を配祀する。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）、[[行教]]が[[宇佐神宮|宇佐]]から[[石清水八幡宮|石清水]]へ八幡神を勧請する途中で宇部郷琴芝の浦に行宮が設けられ、その跡地に村人が八幡神の分霊を祀ったのが当社の起源であるという。[[天授 (日本)|天授]]3年（[[1377年]]）、[[神託]]により現在地の琴崎に遷座した。歴代領主の[[厚東氏]]・[[大内氏]]・[[毛利氏]]らの崇敬を受けた。 [[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）に郷社に列し、[[1938年]]（[[昭和]]13年）に県社に昇格した。[[1966年]]（昭和41年）7月、神社本庁の別表神社に加列された。 == 交通アクセス == ; 車 * [[山陽自動車道]][[宇部インターチェンジ|宇部IC]]下車、約3分。 * [[山口宇部空港]]から約15分。 * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[新山口駅]]から約20分。 == 外部リンク == * [http://kotozaki.com 琴崎八幡宮 公式サイト] * [http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html 琴崎八幡宮 壱千七百年 式年大祭] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことさきはちまんくう}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:宇部市]]

{{神社 |名称=琴崎八幡宮 |画像 = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|260px]] |所在地=山口県宇部市上宇部大小路 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=品陀和気命
気長足比女命
足仲津比古命 |社格=県社・別表神社 |創建=[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]） |例祭=[[10月15日]] }} '''琴崎八幡宮'''（ことざきはちまんぐう）は、[[山口県]][[宇部市]]にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。宇部市の総鎮守として崇敬を受ける。 [[八幡神]]（[[応神天皇|品陀和気命]]・[[神功皇后|気長足比女命]]・[[仲哀天皇|足仲津比古命]]）を主[[祭神]]とし、[[宗像三女神]]（多紀理比女命・多紀津比女命・[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島比女命]]）を配祀する。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）、[[行教]]が[[宇佐神宮|宇佐]]から[[石清水八幡宮|石清水]]へ八幡神を勧請する途中で宇部郷琴芝の浦に行宮が設けられ、その跡地に村人が八幡神の分霊を祀ったのが当社の起源であるという。[[天授 (日本)|天授]]3年（[[1377年]]）、[[神託]]により現在地の琴崎に遷座した。歴代領主の[[厚東氏]]・[[大内氏]]・[[毛利氏]]らの崇敬を受けた。 [[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）に郷社に列し、[[1938年]]（[[昭和]]13年）に県社に昇格した。[[1966年]]（昭和41年）7月、神社本庁の別表神社に加列された。 == 交通アクセス == ; 車 * [[山陽自動車道]][[宇部インターチェンジ|宇部IC]]下車、約3分。 * [[山口宇部空港]]から約15分。 * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[新山口駅]]から約20分。 == 外部リンク == * [http://kotozaki.com 琴崎八幡宮 公式サイト] * [http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html 琴崎八幡宮 壱千七百年 式年大祭] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことさきはちまんくう}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:宇部市]] [[Category:宇部市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2022年12月29日 (木) 02:07 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称={{ill|Kotozaki Hachiman Shrine|ja|琴崎八幡宮}} |画像 = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|260px]] |所在地={{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}{{ill|Ube|ja|宇部市}}上宇部大小路 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|品陀和気命}}
{{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime|ja|気長足比女命}}
{{ill|Chūai|ja|足仲津比古命}} |社格={{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}・{{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} |創建={{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}}元年（{{ill|859|ja|859年}}） |例祭={{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} }}[[Category:Shinto]] Kotozaki Hachimangu}}[[Category:original pages]] Kotozaki Hachimangu[[Category:Shinto]] Kotozaki Hachimangu}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto]] Kotozaki Hachimangu}}[[Category:original pages]] Kotozaki Hachimangu[[Category:Shinto]] Kotozaki Hachimangu}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto]] Kotozaki Hachimangu}}[[Category:original pages]] Kotozaki Hachimangu[[Category:Shinto]] Kotozaki Hachimangu}}[[Category:original pages]] '''Kotozaki Hachiman Shrine'''（琴崎八幡宮[[ja:琴崎八幡宮]]）は、{{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}{{ill|Ube|ja|宇部市}}にある{{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}}。旧{{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}の社格は{{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}で、現在は{{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}の{{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}}。宇部市の総鎮守として崇敬を受ける。 {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}}（{{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|品陀和気命}}・{{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime|ja|気長足比女命}}・{{ill|Chūai|ja|足仲津比古命}}）を主{{ill|Kami|ja|祭神}}とし、{{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}（{{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理比女命}}・{{ill|Tatsutsuhime|ja|多紀津比女命}}・{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比女命}}）を配祀する。 == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}}元年（{{ill|859|ja|859年}}）, {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教}} established a temporary palace at Koteshima Bay in Ube during his journey from {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水}} to enshrine Hachiman. Villagers enshrined the divided spirit of Hachiman on the site. In {{ill|Tenchū 3|ja|天授3年}}（{{ill|1377|ja|1377年}}）, following an oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current location at Kotozaki. It received the patronage of successive feudal lords, including the {{ill|Kuto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and the {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}. In {{ill|1873|ja|明治6年}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, and in {{ill|1938|ja|昭和13年}}, it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}. In July {{ill|1966|ja|昭和41年}}, it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. == Transportation Access == ; By Car * About 3 minutes from {{ill|Sanyo Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}{{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅}} by {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. == External Links == * [http://kotozaki.com 琴崎八幡宮 公式サイト] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=琴崎八幡宮 壱千七百年 式年大祭 |date=20190101000000}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} ことさきはちまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kotozaki Hachimangu]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand Festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotozaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kotozaki Hachimangu]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]]

In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotozaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kotozaki Hachimangu]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]] [[Category:Original page]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia18]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotozaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kotozaki Hachimangu]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]] [[Category:Original page]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia18]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]]

Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotozaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kotozaki Hachimangu]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]]

Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotozaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kotozaki Hachimangu]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kotozaki Hachimangu]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kotozaki Hachimangu]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kotozaki Hachimangu]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]]

It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotozaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotozaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotozaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Kotozaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Kotozaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Kotozaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Kotozaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Kotozaki Hachimangu}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]] [[Category:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[sn:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|12=draft}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity|12=draft}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan|12=draft}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|12=draft}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|12=draft}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity|12=draft}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|12=draft}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa|12=draft}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu|12=draft}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏|12=draft}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道|12=draft}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport|12=draft}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station|12=draft}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Kotozaki Hachimangu}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]] [[Category:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[sn:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]]
