{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|12=draft}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity|12=draft}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan|12=draft}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|12=draft}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|12=draft}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity|12=draft}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|12=draft}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa|12=draft}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu|12=draft}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏|12=draft}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道|12=draft}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport|12=draft}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station|12=draft}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Kotozaki Hachimangu}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]] [[Category:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[sn:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]]

It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity|12=draft}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|12=draft}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa|12=draft}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu|12=draft}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏|12=draft}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道|12=draft}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport|12=draft}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station|12=draft}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Kotozaki Hachimangu}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]] [[Category:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] }} [[en:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[sn:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|12=draft}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity|12=draft}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan|12=draft}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|12=draft}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|12=draft}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity|12=draft}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishimahime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|12=draft}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa|12=draft}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu|12=draft}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏|12=draft}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道|12=draft}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport|12=draft}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station|12=draft}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Kotozaki Hachimangu}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]] [[Category:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[en:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]]

Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|12=draft}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity|12=draft}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan|12=draft}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|12=draft}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|12=draft}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity|12=draft}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishima-hime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|12=draft}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa|12=draft}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu|12=draft}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏|12=draft}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道|12=draft}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport|12=draft}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station|12=draft}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Kotozaki Hachimangu}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|琴崎八幡宮|version=102012503|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]] [[Category:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] }} [[sn:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[en:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]]

Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|qq=draft}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity|qq=draft}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=draft}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan|qq=draft}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|qq=draft}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|qq=draft}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity|qq=draft}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=draft}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishima-hime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|qq=draft}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu|qq=draft}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏|qq=draft}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道|qq=draft}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport|qq=draft}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station|qq=draft}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|琴崎八幡宮|version=102012503|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]] [[Category:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] }} [[sn:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[en:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube, Yamaguchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|qq=}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era (Japan)|qq=}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube, Yamaguchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|qq=}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishima-hime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōkyō|qq=}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōuchi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyō Expressway|qq=}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Ube Airport|qq=}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin-Yamaguchi Station|qq=}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|琴崎八幡宮|version=102012503|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]] [[Category:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[en:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube, Yamaguchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|qq=}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Ōjin)|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era (Japan)|ja|貞観 (日本)|lt=Jōgan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era (Japan)|qq=}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|ja|宇部市|lt=Ube City|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube, Yamaguchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|lt=Hachiman deity|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|qq=}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishima-hime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōkyō|qq=}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|lt=Usa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|lt=Iwashimizu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōuchi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|ja|毛利氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri clan|qq=}}—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陽自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyō Expressway|qq=}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|ja|山口宇部空港|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Ube Airport|qq=}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|ja|新山口駅|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin-Yamaguchi Station|qq=}} by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|琴崎八幡宮|version=102012503|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[en:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]]

It enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]]—[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]], and [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto]]—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishima-hime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōkyō|qq=}} carried the Hachiman deity from [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa]] to [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu]]. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto clan|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]]—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:San'yō Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]]. * About 15 minutes from [[:en:Yamaguchi Ube Airport|Yamaguchi Ube Airport]]. * About 20 minutes from [[:en:Shin-Yamaguchi Station|Shin-Yamaguchi Station]] by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|琴崎八幡宮|version=102012503|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[en:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]] [[Category:宇部市の建築物]] [[Category:宇部市の歴史]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]]

Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, [[:en:Ube, Yamaguchi|Ube City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan | deity = {{ubl|[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]]: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto]]}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ube, Yamaguchi|Ube City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]], it is now a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]]—[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]], and [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto]]—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishima-hime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōkyō|qq=}} carried the Hachiman deity from [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa]] to [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu]]. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto clan|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]]—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:San'yō Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]]. * About 15 minutes from [[:en:Yamaguchi Ube Airport|Yamaguchi Ube Airport]]. * About 20 minutes from [[:en:Shin-Yamaguchi Station|Shin-Yamaguchi Station]] by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|琴崎八幡宮|version=102012503|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]] [[Category:Buildings in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi�]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] {{Commons category|Kotozaki-hachimangu}}

It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]]—[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]], and [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto]]—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishima-hime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōkyō|qq=}} carried the Hachiman deity from [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa]] to [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu]]. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto clan|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]]—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:San'yō Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]]. * About 15 minutes from [[:en:Yamaguchi Ube Airport|Yamaguchi Ube Airport]]. * About 20 minutes from [[:en:Shin-Yamaguchi Station|Shin-Yamaguchi Station]] by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|琴崎八幡宮|version=102012503|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ube]] [[Category:History of Ube]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]] [[Category:Buildings in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] {{Commons category|Kotozaki-hachimangu}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = [[File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|58|18|N|131|16|13|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, [[:en:Ube, Yamaguchi|Ube City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan | deity = {{ubl|[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]]: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto]]}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 1 (859) | festival = October 15 }} '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ube, Yamaguchi|Ube City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]], it is now a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City. It enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]]—[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]], and [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto]]—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishima-hime) as subsidiary deities. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōkyō|qq=}} carried the Hachiman deity from [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa]] to [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu]]. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto clan|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]]—all venerated it. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:San'yō Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]]. * About 15 minutes from [[:en:Yamaguchi Ube Airport|Yamaguchi Ube Airport]]. * About 20 minutes from [[:en:Shin-Yamaguchi Station|Shin-Yamaguchi Station]] by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|琴崎八幡宮|version=102012503|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]] [[Category:Buildings in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kotozaki-hachimangu}}

In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō. == Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:San'yō Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]]. * About 15 minutes from [[:en:Yamaguchi Ube Airport|Yamaguchi Ube Airport]]. * About 20 minutes from [[:en:Shin-Yamaguchi Station|Shin-Yamaguchi Station]] by car. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}} {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|琴崎八幡宮|version=102012503|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kotozaki Hachimangu]] [[ja:琴崎八幡宮]] [[Category:Buildings in Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kotozaki-hachimangu}}

'''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ube, Yamaguchi|Ube City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]], it is now a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōkyō|qq=|1=Gyōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626127}} carried the Hachiman deity from [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa]] to [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu]]. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto clan|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]]—all venerated it.
In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō.
== Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube Interchange|qq=|1=Ube interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946246}} on the [[:en:San'yō Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]]. * About 15 minutes from [[:en:Yamaguchi Ube Airport|Yamaguchi Ube Airport]]. * About 20 minutes from [[:en:Shin-Yamaguchi Station|Shin-Yamaguchi Station]] by car.

'''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ube, Yamaguchi|Ube City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]], it is now a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City.
It enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]]—[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]], and [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto]]—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishima-hime) as subsidiary deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōkyō|qq=|1=Gyōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626127}} carried the Hachiman deity from [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa]] to [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu]]. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto clan|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]]—all venerated it.
In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō.
== Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube Interchange|qq=|1=Ube interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946246}} on the [[:en:San'yō Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]]. * About 15 minutes from [[:en:Yamaguchi Ube Airport|Yamaguchi Ube Airport]]. * About 20 minutes from [[:en:Shin-Yamaguchi Station|Shin-Yamaguchi Station]] by car.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kotozaki-hachimangu]][[ja:琴崎八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11573763]] Wikidata name: Kotozaki-hachimangu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{Coord|33.971667|N|131.270278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, [[:en:Ube, Yamaguchi|Ube City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan | deity = {{ubl|[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]]: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto]]}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 1 (859) | festival = October 15 | location = {{ill|Ube|WD=Q468765}} }}
'''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ube, Yamaguchi|Ube City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]], it is now a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City.
It enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]]—[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]], and [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto]]—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishima-hime) as subsidiary deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōkyō|1=Gyōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626127}} carried the Hachiman deity from [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa]] to [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu]]. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto clan|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]]—all venerated it.
In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō.
== Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube Interchange|1=Ube interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946246}} on the [[:en:San'yō Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]]. * About 15 minutes from [[:en:Yamaguchi Ube Airport|Yamaguchi Ube Airport]]. * About 20 minutes from [[:en:Shin-Yamaguchi Station|Shin-Yamaguchi Station]] by car.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}}

In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. '''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ube, Yamaguchi|Ube City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]], it is now a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City.
It enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]]—[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]], and [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto]]—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishima-hime) as subsidiary deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōkyō|1=Gyōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626127}} carried the Hachiman deity from [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa]] to [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu]]. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto clan|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]]—all venerated it.
In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō.
== Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube Interchange|1=Ube interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946246}} on the [[:en:San'yō Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]]. * About 15 minutes from [[:en:Yamaguchi Ube Airport|Yamaguchi Ube Airport]]. * About 20 minutes from [[:en:Shin-Yamaguchi Station|Shin-Yamaguchi Station]] by car.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kotozaki-hachimangu]][[ja:琴崎八幡宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kotozaki-hachimangu]][[ja:琴崎八幡宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kotozaki-hachimangu]][[ja:琴崎八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11573763]] Wikidata name: Kotozaki-hachimangu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{Coord|33.971667|N|131.270278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, [[:en:Ube, Yamaguchi|Ube City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan | deity = {{ubl|[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]]: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto]]}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 1 (859) | festival = October 15 | location = {{ill|Ube|WD=Q468765}} }}
'''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ube, Yamaguchi|Ube City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]], it is now a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City.
It enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman deity]]—[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto]], and [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto]]—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishima-hime) as subsidiary deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōkyō|1=Gyōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626127}} carried the Hachiman deity from [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa]] to [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu]]. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto clan|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]]—all venerated it.
In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Jinja Honchō.
== Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube Interchange|1=Ube interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946246}} on the [[:en:San'yō Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]]. * About 15 minutes from [[:en:Yamaguchi Ube Airport|Yamaguchi Ube Airport]]. * About 20 minutes from [[:en:Shin-Yamaguchi Station|Shin-Yamaguchi Station]] by car.

It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman deity}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishima-hime) as subsidiary deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōkyō|1=Gyōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626127}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|WD=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan}}—all venerated it.
In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Association of Shinto Shrines.

'''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|en|Ube, Yamaguchi|WD=Q468765|lt=Ube City|1=Ube, Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City.
It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman deity|1=Hachiman}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishima-hime) as subsidiary deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōkyō|1=Gyōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626127}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa|1=Usa Jingū}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|WD=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan|1=Ōuchi clan}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan|1=Mōri clan}}—all venerated it.
In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Association of Shinto Shrines.

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotozaki Hachiman-gū | image = File:Kotozakihachiman.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | coordinates = {{Coord|33.971667|N|131.270278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kamiiube Ōkōji, {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|en|Ube, Yamaguchi|WD=Q468765|lt=Ube City|1=Ube, Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan | deity = {{ubl|{{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman deity|1=Hachiman}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}}} and the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) | type = Former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 (859) | festival = October 15 | location = {{ill|Ube|WD=Q468765|1=Ube, Yamaguchi}} }}
'''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|en|Ube, Yamaguchi|WD=Q468765|lt=Ube City|1=Ube, Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City.
It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman deity|1=Hachiman}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishima-hime) as subsidiary deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōkyō|1=Gyōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626127}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa|1=Usa Jingū}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|WD=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan|1=Ōuchi clan}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan|1=Mōri clan}}—all venerated it.
In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Association of Shinto Shrines.
== Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube Interchange|1=Ube interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946246}} on the {{ill|San'yō Expressway|en|San'yō Expressway|WD=Q7413195|lt=Sanyō Expressway|1=San'yō Expressway}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|en|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|WD=Q1328906|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport|1=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|en|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|WD=Q1136574|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station|1=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car.

'''Kotozaki Hachiman-gū''' (琴崎八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|en|Ube, Yamaguchi|qid=Q468765|lt=Ube City|1=Ube, Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|qid=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]], it is now a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|qid=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is revered as the sōchinju (general tutelary) of Ube City.
It enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman deity|1=Hachiman}}—{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto (Ōjin)|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}—as its main deities, with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Tagirihime, Tagitsuhime, Ichikishima-hime) as subsidiary deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, in Jōgan 1 (859), the monk {{ill|Gyōkyō|ja|行教|lt=Gyōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōkyō|1=Gyōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11626127}} carried the Hachiman deity from {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|qid=Q715632|lt=Usa|1=Usa Jingū}} to {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qid=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}. Along the way, a temporary lodging (angū) was prepared at Kotetsu no Ura in Ubegō (Ube District), and after departing, the villagers enshrined a divided spirit of Hachiman there, forming the origin of the shrine. In Tenshō 3 (1377), following a divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to its current site at Kotozaki. The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|qid=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan|1=Ōuchi clan}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|qid=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan|1=Mōri clan}}—all venerated it.
In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was Ranked as a gōsha (village shrine), and in 1938 (Shōwa 13) it was elevated to a prefectural shrine. In July 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was included among the Beppyo Shrines of Association of Shinto Shrines.
== Access == ;By car * About 3 minutes from the {{ill|Ube Interchange|ja|宇部インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ube Interchange|1=Ube interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10946246}} on the {{ill|San'yō Expressway|en|San'yō Expressway|qid=Q7413195|lt=Sanyō Expressway|1=San'yō Expressway}}. * About 15 minutes from {{ill|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|en|Yamaguchi Ube Airport|qid=Q1328906|lt=Yamaguchi Ube Airport|1=Yamaguchi Ube Airport}}. * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|en|Shin-Yamaguchi Station|qid=Q1136574|lt=Shin-Yamaguchi Station|1=Shin-Yamaguchi Station}} by car.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External links == * [http://kotozaki.com Official Website (Kotozaki Hachiman-gū)] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/kotozaki1700nen/index.html |title=Kotozaki Hachiman-gū 1700th Anniversary Grand festival |date=20190101000000}}

The successive ruling clans—{{ill|Koto clan|ja|厚東氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|qid=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan|1=Ōuchi clan}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|qid=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan|1=Mōri clan}}—all venerated it.
